mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.0 percent, weak labor market conditions suppress consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 55 percent of periods where UNRATE exceeds 9.0 percent, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio falls below 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly increases in retail sales and housing starts signal broad-based consumer demand recovery, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive changes in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 20-day returns with classification accuracy below 52 percent over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during early-cycle expansions with Rank IC below 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly gains in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and rising wage income, which consistently produce positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS increases show zero predictive power for 20-day forward returns with t-statistic below 1.5 over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 215.0, accelerating inflation expectations force monetary tightening and compress equity valuations, yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL values above 215.0 coincide with positive 60-day returns in greater than 60 percent of observations, or predictive R-squared remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly GDP growth and declining unemployment rates confirm a synchronized economic recovery, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising GDPC1 and falling UNRATE fails to predict positive 60-day returns with out-of-sample hit rate below 50 percent over an 8-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the index of industrial production exceeds 98.0, manufacturing capacity utilization approaches historical norms, supporting corporate profit margins and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO levels above 98.0 fail to predict positive 5-day returns with classification accuracy below 51 percent over 5-year rolling windows.
